flashcards.back: Not Only SQL - databases that handle non-relational data structures

flashcards.front: List the four main types of NoSQL databases
flashcards.back: 1. Document stores 2. Key-value 3. Column-family 4. Graph databases
Distributed systems can only guarantee two of: Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance

flashcards.front: What does BASE stand for in NoSQL?
flashcards.back: Basically Available, Soft state, Eventual consistency

flashcards.front: Give an example of a document database

flashcards.back: Column-oriented storage optimized for aggregate queries

flashcards.front: When would you choose a graph database?
flashcards.back: For complex relationships like social networks or fraud detection
System guarantees consistency will be achieved eventually if no new updates

flashcards.front: How does sharding help in NoSQL?
flashcards.back: Horizontal partitioning of data across multiple servers for scalability

flashcards.front: What is a key advantage of key-value stores?
flashcards.back: High performance for simple read/write operations
Parallel processing of large datasets across distributed clusters
